# GTKX

> GTKX is a modern framework for building native Linux applications using React and GTK. It provides the full range of GTK4, GLib, and Node.js APIs, allowing you to create rich, performant desktop applications with the tools and libraries you already know.

## Core Packages

- `@gtkx/react`: React reconciler, GTK/Adwaita intrinsic elements, render() function, createPortal() function
- `@gtkx/ffi`: TypeScript FFI bindings, GLib type definitions and enums
- `@gtkx/css`: CSS-in-JS styling (Powered by Emotion)
- `@gtkx/testing`: Component testing utilities (Testing Library-style API for GTK)
- `@gtkx/cli`: Project scaffolding (gtkx create), dev server with HMR, and production bundling (gtkx build)
- `@gtkx/mcp`: MCP server for AI-powered app interaction, widget tree inspection, automated testing

## Documentation

- [Introduction](https://gtkx.dev/docs/introduction): What GTKX is, how it works, architecture overview, and target audience
- [Getting Started](https://gtkx.dev/docs/getting-started): Create first app, project structure, basic component patterns
- [FFI Bindings](https://gtkx.dev/docs/ffi-bindings): Importing GTK/GLib bindings, async methods, cancellation, error handling
- [Styling and CSS](https://gtkx.dev/docs/styling): css(), cx(), injectGlobal()
- [Portals](https://gtkx.dev/docs/portals): createPortal(), rendering dialogs and popovers outside the normal component tree
- [Testing](https://gtkx.dev/docs/testing): render(), renderHook(), screen queries and userEvent utilities, testing patterns
- [CLI Reference](https://gtkx.dev/docs/cli): gtkx create, gtkx dev, gtkx build commands, npm scripts, programmatic API
- [MCP Integration](https://gtkx.dev/docs/mcp): AI assistant integration via Model Context Protocol, widget tree inspection, automated UI interaction

## Tutorial: Building a Notes App

- [1. Window & Header Bar](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/1-window-and-header-bar): AdwApplicationWindow, AdwHeaderBar, compound components
- [2. Styling with CSS-in-JS](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/2-styling): @gtkx/css, GTK CSS variables, responsive styling
- [3. Lists & Data](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/3-lists): GtkListView, GtkColumnView, virtualized lists, tree models
- [4. Menus & Shortcuts](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/4-menus-and-shortcuts): GtkMenuButton, keyboard shortcuts, actions
- [5. Navigation & Split Views](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/5-navigation): AdwNavigationSplitView, AdwNavigationView, AdwViewStack
- [6. Dialogs & Animations](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/6-dialogs-and-animations): AdwAlertDialog, portals, AdwTimedAnimation, AdwSpringAnimation
- [7. Settings & Preferences](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/7-settings-and-preferences): GSettings schemas, useSetting hook, preferences dialog
- [8. Deploying](https://gtkx.dev/docs/tutorial/8-deploying): Node.js Single Executable Application (SEA), Flatpak/Snap packaging

## API Reference

- [React Package API](https://gtkx.dev/api/react/)
- [FFI Package API](https://gtkx.dev/api/ffi/)
- [CSS Package API](https://gtkx.dev/api/css/)
- [Testing Package API](https://gtkx.dev/api/testing/)

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/gtkx-org/gtkx): Source code, examples, issue tracker
- [GTK4 Documentation](https://docs.gtk.org/gtk4/): Official GTK4 widget reference and concepts
- [Libadwaita Documentation](https://gnome.pages.gitlab.gnome.org/libadwaita/doc/1-latest/): Official Libadwaita component reference
- [GNOME Human Interface Guidelines](https://developer.gnome.org/hig/): Design patterns and UX guidelines for GNOME apps
